description_long: This is a hand axe featuring a broad, single-edged steel blade with a slightly curved cutting edge and a pointed poll at the top. The blade is securely mounted onto a wooden handle, which is wrapped with a crisscrossed leather grip for improved handling and comfort. The handle is hexagonal in cross-section and tapers slightly towards the base, providing ergonomic support for swinging. The blade has a matte metallic finish with visible wear and a subtle gradient of gray and silver tones, while the handle displays a natural wood grain with brown hues. The axe is compact and appears suitable for one-handed use, making it ideal for tasks such as chopping wood or as a utility tool in urban environments. No visible branding or decorative engravings are present, giving it a utilitarian and robust appearance.
description: A compact hand axe with a broad steel blade and a wooden handle wrapped in leather for grip. The design is utilitarian, with a matte metallic blade and a sturdy, ergonomic handle.
description_view_0: Front view showing the narrow profile of the blade and the full length of the handle with visible leather wrapping.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the broad, curved blade and the pointed poll, as well as the ergonomic curvature of the handle.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the blade and the handle's crisscrossed grip pattern.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the blade's edge profile and the handle's curvature, similar to view 1 but mirrored.
attribute_handle_type: Wooden handle, hexagonal cross-section, slightly curved, wrapped with crisscrossed leather for grip.
attribute_blade_design: Single broad steel blade with a curved edge, pointed poll, and no beard; matte finish with utilitarian geometry.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the blade fitted onto the handle.
